name: safe_agentic_workflow
description: SAFe Agentic Workflow (SAW)-modeled software delivery pipeline: task intake -> Business Systems Analyst requirements gate -> author a task-specific coding workflow (.botcircuits/workflows/coding/<task>_workflow.json) -> run that coding workflow -> QAS validation gate with reroute authority (capped at 3 cycles) -> Release Train Engineer PR shepherd (never fixes code, escalates instead) -> System Architect design review -> automated review pass -> human approval/merge, each rejection path routed back through the coding workflow with the specific findings -> documentation summary.

variables.description: The feature or task the user wants implemented, as originally provided.
The derived, testable acceptance criteria / definition of done for the task.
Whether the acceptance criteria are clear, complete, and testable.
Name of the task-specific coding workflow authored under .botcircuits/workflows/coding/ (e.g. user_login_workflow).
The specific, actionable defect findings (from QAS, the System Architect, automated review, or the human reviewer) the next coding-workflow run must fix. Empty on the first pass.
Number of QAS validation cycles run against the current implementation.
Whether QAS validated the implementation against acceptance_criteria.
Number of System Architect design-review cycles run.
Whether the System Architect approved the architecture/design of the implementation.
Number of automated (lint/style/consistency) review cycles run.
Whether the automated review pass found no blocking issues.
Whether the Release Train Engineer confirmed the PR is ready to move into review.

Category of the human reviewer's rejection: 'implementation' for implementation/coding defects, 'acceptance_criteria' for QAS-level acceptance-criteria issues, 'architecture' for architecture/design issues; empty when approved.

settings.action: Describe the task to implement: feature or problem, specific requirements, acceptance criteria if known, and relevant context (tech stack, constraints, etc.).

As Business Systems Analyst: from task_description (plus any clarification since the last pass), derive acceptance_criteria as a specific, testable definition of done; formalize criteria already present. If requirements are missing, ambiguous, or too vague for testable criteria, set requirements_complete to false and list exactly what is missing or unclear; otherwise set requirements_complete to true. Do not guess at missing requirements.
Answer the specific questions raised by the requirements review above so testable acceptance criteria can be finalized.

As coding-workflow planner, follow the botcircuits-coding-workflow-authoring skill exactly. Derive a slug-safe task name from task_description (e.g. 'implement user login' -> user_login). Write the workflow JSON to .botcircuits/workflows/coding/<task-name>_workflow.json with concrete, ordered implementation steps from acceptance_criteria (e.g. db table design -> backend db config -> API endpoint -> UI form -> tests). Build with `botcircuits workflow build --name <task-name>_workflow`. Reuse an existing built workflow for this exact task if present. Set coding_workflow_name to the workflow's name (e.g. user_login_workflow). Do NOT implement application code in this step.
Set qas_iteration=0, qas_approved=false, architect_iteration=0, architect_approved=false, automated_review_iteration=0, automated_review_passed=false, pr_ready=false, revision_findings="".

Run `botcircuits workflow run --name <coding_workflow_name> --initial-args '{"task_description": <task_description>, "acceptance_criteria": <acceptance_criteria>, "revision_findings": <revision_findings>}'` (JSON-escape values; revision_findings is "" on first pass). Do NOT implement code outside that run. On finish, report what was built or fixed and which acceptance criteria it addresses; if the run failed, state exactly what failed.

As QAS with iteration authority: increment qas_iteration by 1. Validate the implementation against every item in acceptance_criteria. If all met: set qas_approved=true, clear revision_findings. Else: set qas_approved=false and set revision_findings to specific, actionable findings — which criterion failed, which file/layer has the defect, and what must change — precise enough for the coding workflow to fix on its next run.

As Release Train Engineer (PR shepherd; do NOT write, fix, or modify code): summarize changes from the coding workflow runs and confirm QAS sign-off from qas_approved. If qas_approved=true: set pr_ready=true and prepare a PR summary for review. If qas_approved=false (QAS cycle cap reached): set pr_ready=false and write an escalation summary of exactly which acceptance criteria remain unresolved and why.
Compile the QAS findings history and the Release Train Engineer's escalation summary into one report of unresolved acceptance criteria for a human to decide how to proceed.

As System Architect: increment architect_iteration by 1. Review at architecture/design level — component boundaries, integration correctness across touched layers, consistency with existing system design, technical debt introduced (not acceptance-criteria pass/fail). If sound: set architect_approved=true. Else: set architect_approved=false and set revision_findings to specific findings of what must change and where.

Run lint, style, and cross-file consistency checks on all code changed by the coding workflow. Increment automated_review_iteration by 1. If no blocking issues: set automated_review_passed=true. Else: set automated_review_passed=false and set revision_findings to the specific blocking issues.

The PR summary, QAS sign-off status, architecture review, and automated review results are above. Reply 'approve' to merge, or 'reject' with comments explaining what's wrong: say whether it's an implementation/coding defect, an acceptance-criteria (QAS-level) issue, or an architecture/design issue.

conditions.condition: human rejects citing an implementation defect
human rejects citing a coding defect
human rejects citing an acceptance criteria issue
human rejects citing a QAS-level issue
human rejects citing an architecture issue
human rejects citing a design issue

settings.action: Set revision_findings to the human reviewer's rejection comments. Reset qas_iteration=0, qas_approved=false, architect_iteration=0, architect_approved=false, automated_review_iteration=0, automated_review_passed=false, pr_ready=false.

Reset qas_iteration=0, qas_approved=false, architect_iteration=0, architect_approved=false, automated_review_iteration=0, automated_review_passed=false, pr_ready=false; QAS re-validates against the human's acceptance-criteria feedback and downstream gates re-run.

Reset architect_iteration=0, architect_approved=false, automated_review_iteration=0, automated_review_passed=false, pr_ready=false; System Architect re-reviews against the human's architecture feedback and downstream gates re-run.

Write an implementation summary document with sections: 1) What Was Built — feature overview and components delivered; 2) Coding Workflow — coding_workflow_name and its step outline; 3) Architecture — how implemented layers integrate, per the System Architect's findings; 4) Acceptance Criteria Checklist — each acceptance_criteria item and its status; 5) Setup and Deployment Instructions — step-by-step run/deploy guide; 6) Review History — every QAS finding and coding-workflow rerun, every architect finding, every automated review issue, and the human approval decision; 7) Future Improvements. Ground every statement in acceptance_criteria and the actual code and findings from the pipeline.
